descriptions.value: Watch... but do not touch it !!
He is faster than you and me.
In fact, it is useless, but it looks cool.
Get the key. Crack the sun. Jump inside.
Above and beyond.
Different worlds awaits.
Imagination is infinite.
The beams that guides us.
Don't judge the book by it's cover.
Your computer is a backup of your soul, a multi-layered, menu-driven representation of who you are, who you care about, and how you sin.
Computers are like Old Testament gods; lots of rules and no mercy.
Computers creating art is an upsetting concept mostly because of what it means about humans.
Man is still the most extraordinary computer of all.
A computer lets you make more mistakes faster than any invention in human history - with the possible exceptions of hand guns and tequila.
Now they can do all these magic things with computers.
Computers can figure out all kinds of problems, except the things in this world that just don't add up.
Computers are useless. They can only give you answers.
One day ladies will take their computers for walks in the park and tell each other, "My little computer said such a funny thing this morning."
It is only when they go wrong that machines remind you how powerful they are.
One of the best things to come out of the home computer revolution could be the general and widespread understanding of how severely limited logic really is.

descriptions.value: King Farnham loves telling stories, especially ones that involve his children and the wild adventures they often have.
The native lizard people of Glekutsu Village love having visitors, which is why the bus stop near their village is well taken care of.
It's not uncommon to see wild foxes in the forest near Glekutsu Village.  This one seems to be waiting for something.
Naigye Isia and her little niece Cally are on their way to visit the castle, where Cally's father works as a high-ranking soldier.
Rachael Moongrass and her daughter Gloria pose for a promotional photo. It took the photographer many tries to get a good shot because Gloria just wouldn't sit still.
The dawn of the anarchy.
When you have nothing, you have nothing to lose!
This guy is looking your way...
I love the sound of it!
I'm too young for this!
Am I dreaming?
The one who will defeat Zolthan.
This boss circles around the room and fires timed shots.
Learn to run and shoot this boss, or you'll run and die.
Be quick on your feet to dodge this large boss while it rolls and jumps.
Zolthan is a sneaky mage, hiding behind spikes while shooting multiple projectiles.
A man with a few tricks up his sleeve
He may be old, but he's still got some lead in his pencil
He only looks like Legolas.
She's a stranger in a strange land.
The only thing she wants is revenge.
The world's a puzzle; no need to make sense out of it.
Not every puzzle is intended to be solved. Some are in place to test your limits. Others are, in fact, not puzzles at all...
Life is puzzle unsolved.
Leave some mystery to the world and they will remember you forever.
Remember what I give it's a part of the puzzle. What I give is a part of the lise, what I give is a lesson!
Beginning of way
Slash your enemies!
He's watching
Don't get burned!
How to dodge them?

Store all items and sell goods at your beautiful base of operations.

The Tomb of Tattered Dreams is what you get when you spend your inheritance on a stinking tomb.
The Mines of Misery, a place you never really want to be... except for the nice loot.
This point of interest housed alchemists until its destruction during the Reignmaker Era.

Drone researcher
The soldier robot
Hungry and dangerous
The gigantic and monstrous

descriptions.value: Shaman throws the herbs into fire and the words – into us. As we embrace what he is saying, this fire becomes a flaming knife. It makes a cut in our lives right where this night is. From now on, the days to come will not be like the days that were.

They say there were other men long, long ago. They lived here, like we do, but they could do a lot more, than we can. Some think they made the bridges of wood.
But bridges of stone are little twigs of a gigantic stone tree we all live on. Noone can make them – they just grow.

– Strange. It is all trouble in here. But I feel so peaceful in these new lands. You feel peace inside, Tik? Not running, not killing – just picking fish?
– I am not picking fish.
– You are not?
– No.
– But what would you do with the ropey stick then, Tik? If not pick fish?
– I...
– I know! You are picking fish, right?
– I am not picking fish! I've dropped my damn spear in there!
Almost too large to be a man, this taroong is armored with dried up clay. His rage crushes his enemies with a gigantic club. His death – if too fast – brings his father's wrath upon his foes.
– When night come, Ghanayat not sleep. Think. Ghanayat want ask who make recipe: "Why make thing such angry, that need take little girl and hide secret in her whole life?"
The water – giving life, taking lives away – rumbles under our feet. Above the water we are above life.

Some say the Facehorns are as big and powerful, as the Big Spirits are. We do not hunt them – we ask. When we decide to fight them, we know we might perish. And with the killing blow we thank them for letting us win, for nothing happens against their will to those who are so mighty.
